fix nested map target binding

Add CLI and library regressions covering nested map and nested map_values, and simplify the nested map case to exercise implicit current-target binding directly.

fix(failing-dags): clean up dag list layout (#226)

Summary:
- replace the one-column failing DAG table with a compact list layout
- add responsive and dark-mode styling for long DAG identifiers
- update dashboard tests for the new markup and count badge

Rationale:
- the previous table styling made each single-column row look oversized
- a list better matches the content and keeps long DAG IDs readable
- the refreshed tests cover the intended structure without table-only checks

Tests:
- source venv/bin/activate && python -m unittest tests.test_failing_dags
- source venv/bin/activate && gunicorn app:app --bind 127.0.0.1:8000

commit.message:
fix(dispatch): scope shadow serialization to mutations

Narrow dispatch shadow protection to explicit kspec mutation windows. Dispatch no longer serializes the full invocation lifecycle, dispatched kspec commands share a mutation lock file, and invocation-owned task mutations now surface explicit mutation failures when protected writes cannot complete.

commit.message:
chore(release): cut 0.3.1

Summary:
- set the project version to 0.3.1 and add the 0.3.1 changelog
  notes
- add a forced docs alias and tighten simulator, domain, and DC
  moduledocs for the current release state
- keep moved hardware scripts out of the default test sweep and
  rename the UDP transport script so mix test stays clean

Rationale:
- ship the current main changes in the 0.3.x line so
  kino_ethercat can consume them without a dependency range bump
- make the release validation path trustworthy by removing hidden
  ExDoc warnings and accidental hardware script execution during
  mix test

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs.fresh
- mix hex.build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Stop documenting consecutive declarations (#348)

Stop carrying declaration-scan state past the first declaration
after an AutoDoc source comment block. This makes declaration
documentation match the documented immediate-adjacency rule
instead of implicitly attaching the same comment block to later
consecutive declarations.

Add a regression test for consecutive declarations and make the
general worksheet fixture spell out the later category
declarations explicitly so it no longer depends on the old buggy
behavior.

AI-assisted with Codex for implementation, test updates, and
changelog entry.

Fixes #169

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add dispatcher-owned bootstrap configuration and cached workspace bootstrap state. Dispatch now runs bootstrap before invocation, reuses prior successful state when valid, records explicit invalidation reasons, and blocks tasks on actionable bootstrap failures.

- update the root README hardware guidance to show the required
  MIX_ENV=test wrapper for scripts moved under test/integration/
  hardware
- switch the README changelog reference to a stable GitHub URL and
  remove the duplicated usage_rules block from AGENTS.md

Rationale:

commit.message:
feat(dispatch): add explicit cross-agent scheduler

Implement an explicit dispatch candidate scheduler across agents.
Rank candidates by band, numeric priority, continuity affinity,
and FIFO fairness while excluding blocked, dependency-blocked,
and stale-workspace continuations before spawn.

Task: @01KKFY9JJ9SDWB05ZBQSV2VBDW
Spec: @dispatch-scheduling-priority-model
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(repo): remove unused workflow scaffolding

Summary:
- delete the unused WORKFLOW.md orchestration template
- remove the empty default config/config.exs file

Rationale:
- the workflow template was not referenced by the repo and duplicated
  external orchestration instructions rather than project guidance
- the config stub only imported Config and was no longer needed once
  the config directory was removed

Validation:
- not run (repo cleanup only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(hardware): move examples into integration suite

Summary:
- move the maintained real-hardware scripts from examples/ into
  test/integration/hardware/scripts and replace the README entrypoint
  with test/integration/hardware/README.md
- add shared hardware ring builders in test/integration/support/
  hardware.ex and reuse them from the hardware test and migrated
  scripts
- update README, changelog, and execution-plan docs to point at the
  new hardware script paths and MIX_ENV=test entrypoints

Rationale:
- keep removable examples/ content alongside the integration support
  code it depends on so the test tree is the single home for hardware
  validation assets
- remove duplicated inline driver and ring setup definitions from the
  maintained scripts in favor of the current shared support drivers
- preserve the maintained real-hardware workflows while making the
  examples/ directory removable

Validation:
- mix format test/integration/support/hardware.ex
  test/integration/hardware/ring_test.exs
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/*.exs
- mix test test/integration/hardware/ring_test.exs --exclude hardware
- MIX_ENV=test mix run -e 'Path.wildcard("test/integration/hardware/
  scripts/*.exs") |> Enum.sort() |> Enum.each(fn path -> File.read!(path)
  |> Code.string_to_quoted!(file: path) end)'
- ETHERCAT_INTERFACE=enp0s31f6 mix test
  test/integration/hardware/ring_test.exs --include hardware
- MIX_ENV=test mix run test/integration/hardware/scripts/scan.exs
  --interface enp0s31f6
- MIX_ENV=test mix run test/integration/hardware/scripts/sdo_debug.exs
  --interface enp0s31f6
- MIX_ENV=test mix run test/integration/hardware/scripts/el3202.exs
  --interface enp0s31f6
- MIX_ENV=test mix run test/integration/hardware/scripts/wiring_map.exs
  --interface enp0s31f6 --settle-ms 20
- MIX_ENV=test mix run
  test/integration/hardware/scripts/multi_domain.exs
  --interface enp0s31f6 --run-s 1 --cross-samples 4

commit.message:
docs(simulator): clarify test fixture and hardware guidance

Summary:
- document that telemetry-triggered helper injections should complete
  before the matching callback returns
- expand the simulator testing guidance with real-hardware positioning,
  scenario granularity, and fixture-tier recommendations
- spell out when to use synthetic fixtures, captured real-device
  fixtures, and hardware runs

Rationale:
- the simulator README should capture the current testing policy instead
  of leaving it implicit in recent code and review discussions
- keeping this guidance next to the simulator design makes future test
  additions more consistent and reduces churn over fixture choice

Validation:
- not run (docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(test): make telemetry-triggered scenario faults synchronous

Summary:
- route telemetry-triggered scenario injections through a per-scenario
  task supervisor instead of a bare spawned process
- wait for the triggered injection to finish before returning from the
  matching telemetry callback and surface timeout or task-exit failures
- add a focused regression test and document the stronger helper
  guarantee in the simulator test README

Rationale:
- the previous spawn-based helper only guaranteed "eventually after the
  event" and left follow-up assertions exposed to process-scheduling
  races
- the current scenarios want causal ordering from the telemetry match to
  the injected fault without promoting master events into simulator-core
  milestones

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/support/scenario_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(dispatch): reevaluate legacy publication mode

Recompute publication mode for reopened pending/in-progress dispatch workspaces from current repository capabilities instead of preserving a legacy manual_merge fallback. Add a regression test covering legacy registry reload in a PR-capable repository.

Task: @01KKFY9F0VXEG6YWVBA20N7DJ2

Spec: @dispatch-branch-integration-contract

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(simulator): add event-triggered fault scenarios

Summary:
- allow nested schedulable simulator faults so milestone and timer
  chains can defer faults until their final step
- add scenario helpers and coverage for telemetry-triggered follow-up
  faults, including the new recovering-entry SAFEOP scenario
- add the captured EL3202 support driver with startup mailbox config
  and writable simulator objects, and widen the startup timeout budget

Rationale:
- the new scenarios need simulator-side chained scheduling plus a small
  test DSL for arming faults from master-observed events
- a realistic EL3202 fixture lets the simulator boot path exercise real
  mailbox startup writes instead of only synthetic drivers
- the startup response-timeout scenario needs more than 2.5s under full
  suite load because it includes a full mailbox timeout before the
  master reports activation failure

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- MIX_ENV=test mix run -e '<EL3202 boot validation>'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(parser): make YAML persistence atomic

Prevent readers from observing partially rewritten YAML files during dispatch workspace registry updates, and tighten the foreground daemon shutdown test wait so full-suite verification is repeatable.

Task: @01KKFY9ECRCS3GRXNRV1NNXY8F
Spec: @dispatch-invocation-worktree-isolation

commit.message:
feat(capture): template generated drivers from captured SDOs

Summary:
- let `gen_driver/2` render startup `mailbox_config/1` steps from
  explicitly captured SDO snapshots
- add a capture-map `gen_driver/2` path and a known-device template for
  Beckhoff EL3202 signal naming and decode logic
- align generated simulator signal names with templated driver output

Rationale:
- improve generated drivers where structural capture alone is not enough
  to stand in for curated integration-test fixtures
- keep the change localized to `EtherCAT.Capture` instead of widening
  the simulator or integration support surface
- preserve the generic scaffold path while allowing targeted upgrades
  for known hardware identities

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/capture.ex test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(dispatch): hydrate legacy workspace records

Restore compatibility for dispatch workspace registry entries written before integration target metadata was required. Normalize missing integration target commit, publication mode, and outcome during parser load so duplicate-record validation still runs against older records.

Task: @01KKFY9F0VXEG6YWVBA20N7DJ2
Spec: @dispatch-branch-integration-contract
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(capture): inline generated driver simulator data

Summary:
- make `gen_driver/2` emit self-contained support driver files
- stop writing separate capture artifacts from `gen_driver/2`
- inline capture-derived `definition_options/1` into generated
  simulator modules

Rationale:
- keep generated drivers editable in one place
- reserve `write_capture/2` for explicit raw snapshot output
- avoid extra artifacts and external file dependencies in generated
  drivers

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/capture.ex test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(capture): scaffold integration support drivers

Summary:
- add `EtherCAT.Capture.gen_driver/2` to generate best-effort
  `EtherCAT.Slave.Driver` modules plus companion simulators
- simplify the interactive capture help to point at `gen_driver/2`
  and switch overwrite options to `force: true`
- fix generated capture-backed scaffolds to store relative capture
  paths and cover sibling output directories in tests

Rationale:
- make live hardware capture useful for building integration support
  drivers with a single command
- keep generated scaffolds reviewable while preserving captured
  structure for simulator fallback
- avoid absolute machine-specific capture paths in generated modules

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/capture.ex test/ethercat/capture_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(master): replace stale reconnect fault reasons

Summary:
- keep runtime_faults and slave_faults aligned when a recovering PDO
  slave reconnects, fails its reconnect-time PREOP rebuild, and
  later heals
- add a master regression plus simulator scenario 33 with a
  mailbox-plus-process-data test driver that reproduces the
  stale disconnect fault case
- document the bugfix in the changelog and the scenario note

Rationale:
- a stale {:down, :disconnected} runtime fault could outlive a
  newer PREOP configuration failure and keep the master stuck
  in :recovering after the slave returned to :op
- recovery retries need to replace and clear both tracked fault
  views together so master state matches the live bus state

commit.message:
Reject optional RunOptions wrappers

Keep Program runner overloads strict when callers forward
`RunOptions | undefined`. Optional wrapper signatures can hide
non-empty contexts, so they now fail overload resolution instead of
silently narrowing to a synchronous return type.

Add a regression test that fixes this contract in place.

commit.message:
fix(evals): long eval string score persistence in MySQL (#494)

* fix(mysql): allow long eval string scores

Summary:
- widen generation_scores.score_string to use MySQL TEXT
- add schema and MySQL-backed regressions for long string score values

Rationale:
- llm_judge and eval ingest can emit arbitrary string scores that
  overflowed the legacy VARCHAR(255) column and caused Error 1406
- keeping score mapping and query behavior unchanged limits the fix to
  the storage boundary and migration path

Tests:
- go test ./sigil/internal/storage/mysql -run
  'TestGenerationScoreModelUsesTextColumnForStringScores|
  TestScoreToModelKeepsLongStringScores|
  TestEvalStoreScoreCRUDAndLatest|
  TestEvalStorePersistsLongStringScores' -count=1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(mysql): migrate legacy score_string columns

Summary:
- inspect the live generation_scores.score_string column during startup
- explicitly alter legacy VARCHAR(255) columns to MySQL TEXT
- add a migration regression that upgrades a downgraded legacy column

Rationale:
- changing the GORM tag only fixes new table creation and does not
  reliably widen existing MySQL columns in place
- existing deployments hitting Error 1406 need an explicit migration
  step before long string evaluator scores can persist safely

Tests:
- go test ./sigil/internal/storage/mysql -run
  'TestGenerationScoreModelUsesTextColumnForStringScores|
  TestScoreToModelKeepsLongStringScores|
  TestEvalStoreScoreCRUDAndLatest|
  TestEvalStorePersistsLongStringScores|
  TestAutoMigrateUpgradesLegacyGenerationScoreStringColumn' -count=1

non-empty tuples still return Promise, while general context array

skip the context path for an empty array.

non-empty dynamic context arrays to keep the overloads aligned with
runImpl().

fix(review-loop): harden reviewer output contracts

Summary:
- Make reviewer launch manifests the authoritative round contract with
  baseline repo state, declared output paths, and a repo-observable
  ownership boundary
- Fail review aggregation/finalization closed on missing reviewer output,
  preserve explicit manual-fallback reasons, and treat reviewer output
  drift or repo state drift as contract violations
- Expand reviewer-loop docs, regression coverage, and completed harness
  history for issues #11 and #24

Rationale:
- Review rounds previously aggregated only the artifacts they were handed,
  so missing reviewer output and side-effect drift could slip through
  without explicit contract failures
- The harness needs lightweight, legible enforcement that matches the
  shipped boundary without overclaiming full runtime isolation

Summary:

  body, and base updates through `gh api` when `gh pr edit` fails with
  `missing required scopes [read:project]`

commit.message:
Tighten Program RunOptions overloads

Restore sound Program runner overloads when callers forward values typed
as RunOptions. Exact RunOptions variables now use dedicated fallback
overloads, while statically context-free option shapes keep the precise
synchronous signatures.

The plain Program overloads also reject unknown RunOptions keys again,
so object literal typos do not silently bypass excess-property checks.
Regression tests cover both the dynamic RunOptions contexts case and
typo rejection across run(), runSync(), and runAsync().

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/163#discussion_r2924080201
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/163#discussion_r2924087970

Summary:
- wire Linear state transitions into the runtime and configure dispatch
  and handoff states from WORKFLOW.md
- bootstrap issue workspaces, expose Codex stderr and selective runtime
  diagnostics, and reduce noisy streaming logs

  relying on prompt compliance or internal runner continuation loops

  and protocol progress during live Codex sessions

  orchestrator to schedule explicit follow-up passes through workflow
  state

commit.message:
refactor(plugin): make saved conversation ordering explicit (#448)

* refactor(plugin): make saved conversation ordering explicit

Replace implicit reverse-based ordering in GenerationPicker with explicit created_at descending sorting to avoid incorrect UI order when API response order changes.

Add focused unit tests for ordering, non-mutation, and invalid timestamp handling.

* fix: guard against NaN in timestamp comparator tie-breaking

When both timestamps are invalid, toTimestamp returns NEGATIVE_INFINITY
for each, and the subtraction yields NaN. Since NaN !== 0 is true in JS,
the comparator returned NaN instead of falling through to the intended
index-based tie-breaker. Add an explicit Number.isNaN guard so that
invalid-timestamp ties correctly reach the deterministic index fallback.

Applied via @cursor push command

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

  overlapping fault lifecycles are readable on failure
- update the simulator and integration READMEs to include the new case
  and move the next direction to a fully scripted variant

Rationale:
- the disconnect variant proves the master can keep an existing mailbox
  fault visible while handling a separate transport-driven recovery path
- readable traces matter more as the scenarios overlap multiple fault
  classes and slave states in one test

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/31_reconnect_preop_mailbox_and_disconnect_mix_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
